2604.10164.title: Inductive Reasoning for Temporal Knowledge Graphs with Emerging Entities
2604.10164.paper_summary: Reasoning on Temporal Knowledge Graphs (TKGs) is essential for predicting future events and time-aware facts. While existing methods are effective at capturing relational dynamics, their performance is limited by a closed-world assumption, which fails to account for emerging entities not present in the training. Notably, these entities continuously join the network without historical interactions. Empirical study reveals that emerging entities are widespread in TKGs, comprising roughly 25\% of all entities. The absence of historical interactions of these entities leads to significant performance degradation in reasoning tasks. Whereas, we observe that entities with semantic similarities often exhibit comparable interaction histories, suggesting the presence of transferable temporal patterns. Inspired by this insight, we propose TransFIR (Transferable Inductive Reasoning), a novel framework that leverages historical interaction sequences from semantically similar known entities to support inductive reasoning. Specifically, we propose a codebook-based classifier that categorizes emerging entities into latent semantic clusters, allowing them to adopt reasoning patterns from similar entities. Experimental results demonstrate that TransFIR outperforms all baselines in reasoning on emerging entities, achieving an average improvement of 28.6% in Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) across multiple datasets. The implementations are available at https://github.com/zhaodazhuang2333/TransFIR.
2604.10164.paper_summary_zh: 推理時間知識圖（TKGs）對於預測未來事件和時間感知事實至關重要。雖然現有方法在捕捉關係動態方面有效，但其性能受到封閉世界假設的限制，這無法考慮訓練中未出現的新興實體。值得注意的是，這些實體不斷加入網絡，卻沒有歷史互動。實證研究顯示，新興實體在TKGs中廣泛存在，約佔所有實體的25%。這些實體缺乏歷史互動，導致推理任務中的性能顯著下降。然而，我們觀察到具有語義相似性的實體往往表現出可比的互動歷史，這表明存在可轉移的時間模式。受到這一見解的啟發，我們提出了TransFIR（可轉移的歸納推理），這是一個新穎的框架，利用語義相似的已知實體的歷史互動序列來支持歸納推理。具體而言，我們提出了一種基於碼本的分類器，將新興實體分類到潛在的語義集群中，使它們能夠採用來自相似實體的推理模式。實驗結果表明，TransFIR在對新興實體的推理中超越了所有基準，實現了在多個數據集上平均提高28.6%的平均倒數排名（MRR）。實現可在 https://github.com/zhaodazhuang2333/TransFIR 獲得。
2604.10164.author: Ze Zhao et.al.
2604.10164.authors: Ze Zhao, Yuhui He, Lyuwen Wu, Gu Tang, Bin Lu, Xiaoying Gan, Luoyi Fu, Xinbing Wang, Chenghu Zhou
